job_title: Software Engineer 2, Product Engineering - Identity Security
job_nlp_title: Software Engineer 2, Product Engineering - Identity Security
taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Software Engineering, Backend Software Engineer
core_skills: Software development, Large-scale data-intensive systems, AI-native development workflows, Debugging with logs, metrics
skill_match_scores: Software development:1.0, Large-scale data-intensive systems:0.0, AI-native development workflows:1.0, Debugging with logs:0.0, Metrics:0.0
must_have_quals: 2+ years building software applications, Experience productionizing large‑scale, data‑intensive systems, High velocity and creativity in solving technical challenges, Experience & desire to adopt & improve AI-native development workflows, Strong debugging with logs, metrics, and signals, Ability to translate business requirements into high‑quality software, Ability to independently solve complex problems and work cross‑functionally, BS in CS/SE/IS or related field
preferred_quals: Experience with Go and Python, Experience in IAM, non‑human/machine identities, secrets management, workload identities, OAuth/OIDC, SCIM, SSO, or policy engines, Experience in cybersecurity or related industries, Experience with big data, statistics, and ML for identity/behavioral risk modeling
core_responsibilities: Build discovery, classification, monitoring, and protection for machine identities and their agentic actions, Ship guardrails and policy engines that enable safe automation while preventing misuse, escalation, and anomalous behavior, Create high‑availability pipelines that ingest and correlate identity, authorization, and behavioral signals from cloud, SaaS, IAM/SSO, and audit logs, Drive 0→1 iteration: prototype quickly, test assumptions, learn from usage, and scale simple solutions, Collaborate across security, platform, and data teams; write and review technical designs; and participate in core SDLC rituals
job_summary: Abnormal Security is focused on securing identities in the cloud and AI environments. The Software Engineer 2 will build and enhance systems for monitoring and protecting machine identities while collaborating with various teams to ensure secure automation and behavioral intelligence.
